pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it doesnt even feel like mine anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around waiting for something that's never coming back. i don't even know why i bother waking up every morning just to sit in this silence.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope... it's just so much. everything we do, every single thing we feel or struggle with, it's just happening on this tiny speck that doesn't even matter. it feels like such a cosmic joke. we spend all this time trying to find meaning in things that are literally destined to turn into nothing in a few billion years. what's even the point of the struggle?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back today and it's just... over. all the years, the funding, the missed sleep... it was all for nothing. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research and now that it's gone, i'm just empty. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the fading embers of my own soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, and I find I no longer have the strength to face the dawn. The world is so terribly loud, yet so empty. I feel as though I am merely a ghost haunting my own life, waiting for the darkness to finally claim what is left of me. Is there truly any grace left in this world, or is it better to simply fade into the velvet silence of the night?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the funding, the house... all of it. i've spent 6 years building this and now i'm just sitting here in an empty office looking at legal notices. the worst part isnt even the money, it’s that i can’t even look my parents in the eye. i’ve become this massive disappointment and i just feel like there's no way to fix the way people look at me now. i'm just... done.
